scope.summary:
Goal: make the native editors CONSUME the FEMS retrieval capsule (the MemoryPack from Pillar 12) so that relevant project memory surfaces inline while a model or operator is editing a document. This MT is read-only consumption: it adds (a) a typed Rust client to the EXISTING handshake_core FEMS read API and (b) a 'Relevant Memory' side panel that requests a MemoryPack keyed on the active document/selection context and renders provenance-first items, each of which links back to its source through the E5 shared navigation bus. There is NO backend build in this MT — bind the existing handshake_core FEMS read endpoint; if no FEMS read endpoint exists, raise a TYPED BLOCKER and render an empty-state banner. PostgreSQL/EventLedger is the only backing store on the backend side (no SQLite anywhere); this MT does not touch the store directly — it only consumes via the API.

What a MemoryPack is (Pillar 12 contract the client must model): a retrieval capsule bounded to <=500 tokens total and <=24 items, where each item is one of three kinds — episodic (what happened: events, prior sessions, edits), semantic (durable facts/concepts), or procedural (how-to steps, recipes, workflows). Each item is provenance-first: it carries a human-readable summary AND a machine source reference (e.g. a loom:// or atelier:// URI, a document id + range, or an event id) that the navigation bus can resolve to a concrete editor target. The client MUST enforce the <=24-item cap defensively even if the server returns more (truncate + log), and MUST treat the token budget as advisory metadata it surfaces, not something it recomputes.

Files to CREATE:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/fems/memory_client.rs  (~250-350 lines) — defines the typed read client and the deserialized MemoryPack model. Define:
      * enum MemoryKind { Episodic, Semantic, Procedural } (derive Clone, Debug, PartialEq, serde::Deserialize with #[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]).
      * struct MemorySource { uri: Option<String>, document_id: Option<String>, byte_range: Option<(usize, usize)>, event_id: Option<String> } — the provenance reference; at least one field must be present for an item to be navigable (enforce in a validate() method; non-navigable items render but disable the source link).
      * struct MemoryItem { id: String, kind: MemoryKind, summary: String, source: MemorySource, score: Option<f32> }.
      * struct MemoryPack { items: Vec<MemoryItem>, token_estimate: Option<u32>, truncated: bool, context_key: String }.
      * struct MemoryClient { base: ApiClient } where ApiClient is the EXISTING shared backend HTTP/core client already used by other E* MTs — the real module is src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs (reuse it; do NOT create a second HTTP stack). Add async fn fetch_pack(&self, workspace_id: &str, context: &MemoryContext) -> Result<MemoryPack, MemoryClientError>.
      * struct MemoryContext { workspace_id: String, document_id: Option<String>, selection_text: Option<String>, cursor_byte: Option<usize> } — built from the active editor pane's focus + SharedSelection (from the MT-031 shared interaction bus — provided by the real WP-011 modules src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs + src/frontend/handshake_native/src/event_bus.rs).
      * enum MemoryClientError { EndpointMissing, Http(StatusCode-or-equivalent), Decode(String), OverCap { returned: usize } } — EndpointMissing is the TYPED BLOCKER variant: it is returned when the FEMS read route is absent (404 on the documented path, or feature-not-present sentinel). The panel maps EndpointMissing to the empty-state banner; it must NOT panic and must NOT silently no-op.
      * fetch_pack MUST clamp items to 24 after decode (truncate, set truncated=true, log a warning) so the <=24 cap holds regardless of server behavior.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/fems/relevant_memory_panel.rs  (~250-350 lines) — defines RelevantMemoryPanel, an egui side panel widget. Define:
      * struct RelevantMemoryPanel { client: MemoryClient, last_context: Option<MemoryContext>, current: Option<MemoryPack>, in_flight: bool, blocker: Option<MemoryClientError> }.
      * fn show(&mut self, ui: &mut egui::Ui, nav_bus: &NavigationBus) — renders the panel.
      * fn refresh_for_context(&mut self, ctx: MemoryContext) — debounced trigger that requests a new pack when the active document/selection context changes (compare against last_context; skip if unchanged).
      * Rendering: group items by MemoryKind (Episodic / Semantic / Procedural section headers), and within each section render each item as a row: the summary text, a small kind badge, and a 'Go to source' link button. Provenance-first means the source affordance is always visible; clicking it builds a navigation target from MemorySource and calls nav_bus.navigate_to(target) (the E5 shared navigation bus from MT-037, built on the real WP-011 shared bus modules src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs + src/frontend/handshake_native/src/event_bus.rs).
      * Empty-state / blocker: when blocker == Some(EndpointMissing), render a calm empty-state banner ('Relevant Memory unavailable — FEMS read endpoint not present in this build') instead of the list, and surface the typed blocker upward so the WP validator sees it. When the pack is present but items is empty, render a neutral 'No relevant memory for this context' state.

Files to EDIT:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/fems/mod.rs  — add `pub mod memory_client;` and `pub mod relevant_memory_panel;` (create the mod.rs / fems dir if E* prior MTs have not; if the fems module does not yet exist, create src/frontend/handshake_native/src/fems/mod.rs declaring both submodules and re-exporting the public types).
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/lib.rs — add `pub mod fems;` so the new module is part of the handshake_native crate.
  - pane-registry wiring (reuse the WP-011 pane registry from src/frontend/handshake_native/src/pane_registry.rs and the shared command bus from src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs + src/frontend/handshake_native/src/event_bus.rs, wired in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/app.rs): register RelevantMemoryPanel as a dockable pane so it can be opened alongside the active editor. Use the existing pane registry API; do NOT invent a new docking system. Subscribe the panel to focus/selection changes via the MT-031 interaction bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) so refresh_for_context fires on context change.

Exact crates / egui / AccessKit patterns:
  - Reuse the existing async runtime + HTTP/core client the other E* MTs already bind (do not add a second client). Deserialize with serde / serde_json into MemoryPack.
  - egui: render the panel inside the host pane area (the pane registry supplies the egui::Ui); group sections with egui::CollapsingHeader per MemoryKind; render each source link as egui::Button or egui::Link; show in_flight with a egui::Spinner.
  - AccessKit (reuse the WP-011 AccessKit id registry at src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/registry.rs, part of the accessibility/ module): register stable author_ids so swarm agents can drive the panel — author_id="relevant-memory-panel" Role::GenericContainer on the outer frame; author_id="relevant-memory-list" Role::List on the items container; each item row author_id="mem-item-{item.id}" Role::ListItem; each source link author_id="mem-source-{item.id}" Role::Button. Pull ids through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/registry.rs) rather than hardcoding to avoid collisions.

Exact handshake_core API bound: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/pack?context=... (the EXISTING FEMS read endpoint that returns the MemoryPack/retrieval capsule). The client serializes MemoryContext into the query (document_id, selection_text, cursor_byte) and deserializes the MemoryPack response. If this route is ABSENT in the current handshake_core build, fetch_pack returns MemoryClientError::EndpointMissing (the TYPED BLOCKER) — do NOT add the route, do NOT rewrite the backend; record the blocker and ship the empty-state path so the editor remains usable.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. Confirm whether the FEMS read endpoint exists in handshake_core (search the existing API surface / route table the other E* MTs already call). If present, bind it; if absent, implement the EndpointMissing typed-blocker path and the empty-state banner, and record the blocker in the handoff.
  2. Define the MemoryPack model in memory_client.rs exactly to the Pillar 12 shape above (3 kinds, provenance-first source, <=24 items, token estimate as advisory metadata).
  3. Implement fetch_pack: build the request from MemoryContext, call the existing ApiClient, decode, then defensively clamp to 24 items (truncate + truncated=true + log).
  4. Implement RelevantMemoryPanel rendering + the navigation hand-off: build a navigation target from MemorySource (prefer uri, else document_id+byte_range, else event_id) and call the MT-037 navigation bus.
  5. Wire focus/selection -> refresh_for_context through the MT-031 interaction bus; debounce so rapid cursor moves do not spam the endpoint.
  6. Register the panel in the WP-011 pane registry (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/pane_registry.rs) + AccessKit id registry (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/registry.rs).
  7. Tests as in proof_targets — including the missing-endpoint path producing the typed blocker + banner with no panic and no silent no-op.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-011 (rich-text / document editor pane providing the active-document context) must be merged.
  - MT-031 (shared selection/clipboard/command interaction bus) must be merged — supplies the focus + SharedSelection that builds MemoryContext.
  - MT-037 (shared navigation bus) must be merged — supplies nav_bus.navigate_to for provenance source links.
  - The existing shared ApiClient / async runtime used by other E* MTs must be available to reuse.

Risk if missed: the editors would ignore the typed memory pillar (Pillar 12) entirely, and a no-context model would lose the provenance-first memory FEMS is meant to provide inline while editing — defeating the 'relevant memory surfaces where you work' invariant and blocking MT-065 and MT-074 which build on the consumed capsule.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: memory_client parses a MemoryPack containing at least one episodic, one semantic, and one procedural item into the typed MemoryKind enum — proven by cargo test deserializing a fixture JSON.
AC-002: The <=24-item cap is enforced client-side: a fixture with 30 items decodes to a MemoryPack with exactly 24 items, truncated=true, and a logged warning — proven by cargo test.

AC-003: The Relevant Memory panel renders, for a seeded document context, the returned items grouped by kind with a visible provenance 'Go to source' affordance on each navigable item — proven by kittest render + AccessKit dump.

AC-004: Clicking a memory item's source link builds a navigation target from MemorySource and routes to that source via the E5 navigation bus (nav_bus.navigate_to called with the resolved target) — proven by kittest interaction asserting the bus received the target.

AC-005: When the FEMS read endpoint is absent, fetch_pack returns MemoryClientError::EndpointMissing (the typed blocker) and the panel renders the empty-state banner — no panic, no silent no-op — proven by cargo test + kittest.
AC-006: Consumption is read-only: there is no write/POST/PUT/DELETE call to any memory endpoint and no direct store access — proven by grep of the memory_client call sites (GET only).

AC-007: AccessKit nodes present: 'relevant-memory-panel' (GenericContainer), 'relevant-memory-list' (List), and at least one 'mem-item-{id}' (ListItem) with a 'mem-source-{id}' (Button) — proven by AccessKit tree dump in kittest.
AC-008: cargo test -p handshake-native passes across all relevant-memory tests (tests/test_relevant_memory.rs) with no failures and no panics.
proof_targets: PT-001: cargo test -p handshake-native memory_client -- --nocapture => MemoryPack parses episodic+semantic+procedural items; 30-item fixture clamps to 24 with truncated=true; all GREEN.
PT-002: kittest harness: seed a document context, render RelevantMemoryPanel, assert items appear grouped by kind each with a source link affordance.
PT-003: kittest harness: click a memory item source link, assert the navigation bus received the resolved target derived from MemorySource.

PT-004: cargo test + kittest: missing-endpoint path returns MemoryClientError::EndpointMissing and renders the empty-state banner; assert no panic and that a typed blocker (not a silent no-op) is produced.
PT-005: AccessKit tree snapshot: assert nodes 'relevant-memory-panel', 'relevant-memory-list', and a 'mem-item-{id}' + 'mem-source-{id}' pair present with correct roles.
implementation_notes: Reuse the existing shared ApiClient / async runtime that other E* MTs already bind for handshake_core calls; do NOT add a second HTTP stack.
Model the MemoryPack to Pillar 12: <=500 token budget (advisory metadata the client surfaces, never recomputes) and <=24 items (hard cap enforced client-side via defensive truncate).
Provenance-first: every rendered item shows its source affordance; an item with no resolvable MemorySource field renders but disables the source link rather than hiding the item.
Build the navigation target with a clear precedence: prefer MemorySource.uri, else document_id + byte_range, else event_id; pass to the MT-037 navigation bus.
Debounce refresh_for_context on selection/cursor changes (compare against last_context; skip identical contexts) to avoid spamming the read endpoint.
EndpointMissing is a first-class typed outcome, not an error to swallow: surface it to the WP validator via the handoff and render the calm empty-state banner so the editor stays usable.
Pull AccessKit author_ids through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry; register the panel through the WP-011 pane registry and command bus rather than inventing new shell wiring.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/* Front End Memory Panel (memory browser / MemoryPack preview reference) — port the item grouping (episodic/semantic/procedural), provenance source-link affordance, and empty-state pattern into the egui RelevantMemoryPanel
binds_backend_api: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/pack?context=... (existing handshake_core FEMS read endpoint returning the MemoryPack/retrieval capsule). Read-only. If the route is ABSENT in the current build, fetch_pack returns MemoryClientError::EndpointMissing as a TYPED BLOCKER — do not add the route, do not rewrite the backend; gaps are typed blockers.

risks: RISK-001: The FEMS read endpoint may not exist in the current handshake_core build, tempting the coder to add a backend route — that is out of scope and forbidden (no backend rewrite).
RISK-002: The server returns more than 24 items or exceeds the token budget; if the client trusts the server blindly it violates the Pillar 12 caps and bloats the panel.
RISK-003: A MemoryItem with no resolvable MemorySource produces a dead 'Go to source' link or a panic when building the navigation target.
RISK-004: Rapid cursor/selection changes spam the read endpoint (no debounce), causing latency and wasted requests while editing.
RISK-005: Treating EndpointMissing as a silent no-op (empty panel with no signal) hides the gap from the WP validator and from the operator, so the missing pillar goes unnoticed.
RISK-006: A second HTTP/async stack is introduced instead of reusing the shared ApiClient, fragmenting the backend-binding surface.
RISK-007: AccessKit author_id collisions if the panel is mounted in multiple windows or item ids are not unique, breaking swarm addressing.
RISK-008: The panel performs a write/POST against memory, breaking the read-only consumption invariant of this MT.
minimum_controls: MC-001: fetch_pack must defensively clamp to <=24 items after decode (truncate, set truncated=true, log a warning) regardless of server behavior — covered by AC-002.
MC-002: EndpointMissing is a typed error variant that maps to a visible empty-state banner AND is surfaced to the WP validator via the handoff — never a silent no-op (AC-005, PT-004).
MC-003: Items with no resolvable MemorySource render with a disabled source link; navigation-target construction returns None safely instead of panicking (AC-003).
MC-004: refresh_for_context is debounced and skips unchanged contexts to bound endpoint traffic (implementation note enforced).
MC-005: Reuse the existing shared ApiClient/async runtime — no second HTTP stack (AC-006 grep gate confirms GET-only single client).
MC-006: AccessKit author_ids are drawn from the WP-011 id registry and item ids are unique per pack, preventing collisions (AC-007).
MC-007: Static check / grep gate confirms no write verbs (POST/PUT/DELETE) and no direct store access in the memory module — read-only consumption only (AC-006).
